attacking_style: Pure bunch sprinter who rarely attacks, but when launching shows willingness to go from distance (300-400m) rather than wait for perfect positioning. Excels in chaotic, wind-shattered finales requiring bike-handling over pure speed.
team_tactics: Heavily reliant on team control and leadout trains (Jumbo-Visma/Lotto-Dstny). Teams frequently overcommit resources with multi-stage chase efforts. Leadout coordination failures and poor final 500m positioning are recurring issues, especially post-2022.
terrain_preference: Pure flat sprints and wind-shattered stages with echelons. Can handle rising finishes better than pure speed specialists. Exposed and eliminated on any stage with categorized climbs or prolonged uncategorized climbing before finale.
vulnerability: Positioning discipline collapses under pressure—frequently boxed, starts sprints too early, or loses key wheels at barriers. Struggles after long waits between sprint opportunities (form fade). Completely dropped on hillier sprint stages with Cat 2+ climbs.
key_patterns: Launches from unusually long distance (300-400m) when confident, forcing followers into disadvantageous positions (3 instances)
Frequently boxed or compromised in final 500m despite team control, suggesting positioning panic under pressure (7 instances)
Excels in wind-shattered/echelon stages requiring bike-handling over pure speed, winning reduced sprints (4 victories in chaos)
Eliminated entirely on stages with categorized climbs pre-finale, clear terrain vulnerability versus pure sprinters (4 instances)
Team overcommits resources (multi-stage chases) but fails to convert, creating frustration and coordination breakdowns (5 instances)
Form fades noticeably after long gaps between sprint opportunities or extended stage races (3 instances Tour/Dauphiné)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 shows invisible finishes, crashes, and dropped on pace despite numerical advantages. Post-ban 2022 win followed by regression. Leadout coordination broken.
